(14 October 1845 – 29 September 1908) was a French history painter and illustrator.

French painter (1845–1908)

**Albert Pierre René Maignan** (14 October 1845 – 29 September 1908) was a French history painter and illustrator.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Albert_Maignan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

In 1864, he left his hometown to study law in Paris, earning his diploma in 1866. During his studies he also painted and took art lessons from [Jules Achille Noël](/wiki/Jules_Achille_No%C3%ABl "Jules Achille Noël"). He had his début at the [Salon](/wiki/Salon_(Paris) "Salon (Paris)") in 1867, and continued to exhibit there throughout his life. In 1868, he travelled extensively, painting in [Rouen](/wiki/Rouen "Rouen"), [Córdoba](/wiki/C%C3%B3rdoba,_Andalusia "Córdoba, Andalusia"), [Seville](/wiki/Seville "Seville") and at the [Suez Canal](/wiki/Suez_Canal "Suez Canal") before its opening. Upon his return, he found a position in the studios of [Évariste Vital Luminais](/wiki/%C3%89variste_Vital_Luminais "Évariste Vital Luminais").

In 1889, he won a gold medal at the [Exposition Universelle](/wiki/Exposition_Universelle_(1889) "Exposition Universelle (1889)") and received the Medal of Honor at the Salon in 1892. Three years later, he was named a Knight in the [Légion d'honneur](/wiki/L%C3%A9gion_d%27honneur "Légion d'honneur").

Most of his work is devoted to [history painting](/wiki/History_painting "History painting"), although he also produced many portraits. His Spanish and [Orientalist](/wiki/Orientalism "Orientalism") paintings show the influence of [Henri Regnault](/wiki/Henri_Regnault "Henri Regnault"). After 1889, he also drew illustrations and painted decorative murals, including some at the Salon des Lettres at the [Hôtel de Ville](/wiki/H%C3%B4tel_de_ville_de_Paris "Hôtel de ville de Paris") and the [Chamber of Commerce and Industry in Saint-Étienne](/w/index.php?title=Chamber_of_Commerce_and_Industry_in_Saint-%C3%89tienne&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chamber of Commerce and Industry in Saint-Étienne (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chambre_de_commerce_et_d%27industrie_de_Saint-%C3%89tienne_/_Montbrison "fr:Chambre de commerce et d'industrie de Saint-Étienne / Montbrison")]. Branching out, from 1895 to 1899 he created a series of [tapestries](/wiki/Tapestries "Tapestries") for the Salle des Conférences at the [Palais du Luxembourg](/wiki/Palais_du_Luxembourg "Palais du Luxembourg").

He was also part of a group of painters who decorated the foyer at the [Opéra-Comique](/wiki/Op%C3%A9ra-Comique "Opéra-Comique") and was one of several who were given commissions to decorate "[Le Train Bleu](/wiki/Le_Train_Bleu_(restaurant) "Le Train Bleu (restaurant)")", a famous restaurant in the Gare de Lyon, in 1900.
